meaning: perhaps; maybe; perchance; by any chance ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj・phrase
used to express a guess
もしかしたら is always at the beginning of the sentence
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: もしかしたら

examples.japanese: もしかしたら、明日行けないかもしれない。
examples.english: I might not be able to come tomorrow.
examples.pronunciation: moshika shitara, ashita ikenai kamo shirenai.

examples.japanese: もしかしたら病気かもしれない。
examples.english: I might be sick.
examples.pronunciation: moshika shitara byouki kamoshirenai.

examples.japanese: もしかしたら彼は気が変わるかもしれません。
examples.english: He might change his mind.
examples.pronunciation: moshika shitara kare wa ki ga kawaru kamoshire masen.

examples.japanese: もしかすると彼の話はうそかもしれない。
examples.english: It's possible his story may be a lie.
examples.pronunciation: moshika suruto kare no hanashi wa uso kamo shirenai.

examples.japanese: もしかするとあそこに座っている人は有名な人かもしれない。
examples.english: That person sitting there could be a celebrity.
examples.pronunciation: moshika suruto asoko ni suwatte iru hito wa yuumei na hito kamo shirenai.

examples.japanese: もしかしたら彼は会社をやめたかもしれない。
examples.english: Perhaps he has quit his job.
examples.pronunciation: moshika shitara kare wa kaisha o yameta kamoshirenai.

examples.japanese: もしかしたら今日彼女に会えるかもしれない。
examples.english: I might be able to meet her today.
examples.pronunciation: moshika shitara kyou kanojo ni aeru kamoshirenai.

examples.japanese: もしかすると、それは偽物かもしれない。
examples.english: That might be a fake.
examples.pronunciation: moshika suruto, sore wa nisemono kamo shirenai.

meaning: if; in the case; supposing ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: もしも + ... + たら

examples.japanese: もしも明日は晴れたらピクニックに行きましょう。
examples.english: If it's sunny tomorrow, let's go out for a picnic.
examples.pronunciation: moshimo ashita wa haretara pikunikku ni ikimashou.

examples.japanese: もしも日本に行けたら、大阪の友達に会いたいです。
examples.english: If I could go to Japan, I would like to meet my friends in Osaka.
examples.pronunciation: moshimo nihon ni iketara, oosaka no tomodachi ni aitai desu.

examples.japanese: もしも試験を受けたら、合格したいです。
examples.english: In the case that I take the exam, I would like to pass.
examples.pronunciation: moshimo shiken o uketara, goukaku shitai desu.

examples.japanese: もしも地震が起きたら、この家は丈夫だから倒れない。
examples.english: In the case that an earthquake happens, this house is strong and will not collapse.
examples.pronunciation: moshimo jishin ga okitara, kono ie wa joubu da kara taorenai.

examples.japanese: もしも日本へ留学しなかったら、今ごろは結婚しているかもしれない。
examples.english: If I hadn't gone to study abroad in Japan, I might have been married by now.
examples.pronunciation: moshimo nihon e ryuugaku shinakattara, imagoro wa kekkon shite iru kamo shirenai.

examples.japanese: もしも時間があったら、ダンスを習いたいです。
examples.english: If I had the time, I would like to learn to dance.
examples.pronunciation: moshimo jikan ga attara, dansu o naraitai desu.

examples.japanese: もしも私が本当のことを話したら、彼は怒るだろう。
examples.english: If I tell the truth, he will get angry.
examples.pronunciation: moshimo watashi ga hontou no koto o hanashi tara, kare wa okoru darou.

examples.japanese: もしも願いが１つだけ叶うとしたら、どんな願い事をしますか。
examples.english: If you could have any 1 wish granted, what would you wish for?
examples.pronunciation: moshimo negai ga hitotsu dake kanau to shitara, donna negaigoto o shimasu ka.

meaning: and; also; as well; either/or; neither/nor
related:
listing of attributes:
だけでなく
はもちろん
ばかりでなく
も
上に
とか～とか
し
さらに
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
"not only but also"
shows that expectations were surpassed
lists two things

examples.japanese: 本を読むのが好きな人もいれば、嫌いな人もいます。
examples.english: If there are people who like to read books, there will also be people who dislike it.
examples.pronunciation: hon o yomu no ga sukina hito mo ireba, kiraina hito mo imasu.

examples.japanese: 学生の態度もわるければ教師の態度もわるい。
examples.english: If the student's attitude is bad, then the teacher's attitude will also be bad.
examples.pronunciation: gakusei no taido mo warukereba kyoushi no taido mo warui.

examples.japanese: 人生は楽しいこともあれば、苦しいこともある。
examples.english: In life, there are both ups and downs.
examples.pronunciation: jinsei wa tanoshii koto mo areba, kurushii koto mo aru.

examples.japanese: 彼は日本語もできれば英語もできる。
examples.english: He can speak both English and Japanese.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa nihongo mo dekireba eigo mo dekiru.

examples.japanese: あのメキシコ料理屋の食べ物は、値段も安ければ味もいいです。
examples.english: That Mexican restaurant has cheap prices and also the food tastes good.
examples.pronunciation: ano mekishiko ryouriya no tabemono wa, nedan mo yasu kereba aji mo ii desu.

examples.japanese: 私の彼氏はタバコも吸わなければ、お酒も飲まない。
examples.english: My boyfriend neither smokes nor drinks.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no kareshi wa tabako mo suwana kereba, osake mo nomanai.

examples.japanese: どこかへ旅行に行きたいけど、時間もなければ、お金もない。
examples.english: I want to go travelling somewhere, but I have neither time nor money.
examples.pronunciation: doko ka e ryokou ni ikitai kedo, jikan mo nakereba, okane mo nai.

examples.japanese: このバッグは値段も手頃なら、デザインもかわいくてすぐ買ってしまった。
examples.english: This bag has reasonable price and good design so I bought it right away.
examples.pronunciation: kono baggu wa nedan mo tegoro nara, dezain mo kawaikute sugu katte shimatta.

examples.japanese: 父はお酒も飲めばタバコも吸うので、健康が心配だ。
examples.english: My father not only drinks wine but also smokes. I’m really worried about his health.
examples.pronunciation: chichi wa osake mo nomeba tabako mo suu node, kenkou ga shinpai da.

examples.japanese: カレンダーは真っ白だった。書き込みもなければ、印もなかった。
examples.english: The calendar is completely blank. There were no memos or marks written next to any of the dates on the calendar.
examples.pronunciation: karendaa wa masshiro datta. kakikomi mo nakereba, shirushi mo nakatta.

meaning: there is no way to; it’s impossible to ~
related:
わけがない
impossible to
がたい
わけがない
ようがない
Opposite situation -> Other party thinks Subject is not capeable to do something, and Subject denies it :
ないとはない 
(something) never happens; there is no possibility that
ことはない 
very unlikely to/showing no signs of
そうもない /そうにない
cannot possibly be; hardly とても～ない
thats impossible まさか
lack of other options: しかたがない
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（ます形）ます + ようがない / ようもない
usageJapanese:
"〜できない / 〜したくても手段がない"
不可能であることを強調して言う時に使う。

examples.japanese: どうにもしようがない。
examples.english: Nothing can be done about it.
examples.pronunciation: dou ni mo shiyou ga nai.

examples.japanese: お礼の申し上げようもない。
examples.english: I cannot thank you enough.
examples.pronunciation: orei no moushiage you mo nai.

examples.japanese: 台風で電車もバスも動かないので会社へ行きようがない。
examples.english: During the typhoon, the trains and busese are not operating, so I have no way to get to work.
examples.pronunciation: taifuu de densha mo basu mo ugokanai node kaisha e iki you ga nai.

examples.japanese: 何とも言いようがないなあ。
examples.english: I just don't know what to say.
examples.pronunciation: nanto mo ii you ga nai naa.

examples.japanese: 彼女の住所も電話番号も分からないので、連絡の取りようがない。
examples.english: I don’t have her address or phone number so I have no way to contact her.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo no juusho mo denwabangou mo wakaranai node, renraku no toriyou ga nai.

examples.japanese: 携帯電話もお金も盗まれてしまって、連絡のしようがない。
examples.english: My cellphone and money was all stolen, so I have no way to contact anyone.
examples.pronunciation: keitai denwa mo okane mo nusumarete shimatte, renraku no shiyou ga nai.

examples.japanese: 意見を求められたが、昨日の会議の内容が全然わからないので、答えようがない。
examples.english: I was asked for my opinion, but I didn't understand the content of yesterday's meeting and I cannot give an answer.
examples.pronunciation: iken o motometa ga, kinou no kaigi no naiyou ga zenzen wakaranai node, kotae you ga nai.

examples.japanese: 彼女の作文は、日本語がめちゃくちゃで直しようがない。
examples.english: The Japanese in her essay is all over the place, and there is no way of fixing it.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo no sakubun wa, nihongo ga mechakucha de naoshiyou ga nai.

examples.japanese: あの男が浮気したから二人の関係はもう元に戻りようがない。
examples.english: That man cheated on her, so there is no way for their relationship to return to normal.
examples.pronunciation: ano otoko ga uwaki shita kara futari no kankei wa mou moto ni modori you ga nai.

meaning: not try to; not make an effort to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（意向形） + ようとしない

examples.japanese: 彼は早く寝ようとしない。
examples.english: He doesn't try to go to bed early.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa hayaku neyou to shinai.

examples.japanese: その子供はどうしても野菜を食べようとしない。
examples.english: That kid will not even try to eat vegetables.
examples.pronunciation: sono kodomo wa doushitemo yasai o tabeyou to shinai.

examples.japanese: 彼は自分が間違っていることを認めようとしない。
examples.english: He refuses to recognize that he's wrong.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa jibun ga machigatteiru koto o mitomeyou to shinai.

examples.japanese: 彼は自分のことは何も言おうとしない。
examples.english: He doesn’t talk much about himself.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa jibun no koto wa nani mo iou to shinai.

examples.japanese: うちの息子は夢をあきらめようとしない。
examples.english: My son isn’t giving up on his dream.
examples.pronunciation: uchi no musuko wa yume o akirameyou to shinai.

examples.japanese: 彼と話しても無駄だ。聞こうとしないから。
examples.english: It's useless to talk to him. He doesn't even attempt to listen.
examples.pronunciation: kare to hanashitemo muda da. kikou to shinai kara.

examples.japanese: 仕事がたくさん残っているけど、誰も何もしようとしないわね。
examples.english: There's a lot of work left to do, but no one is making any effort to do anything..
examples.pronunciation: shigoto ga takusan nokotte iru kedo, dare mo nani mo shiyou to shinai wa ne.

examples.japanese: どうして自分のミスを認めようとしないの？
examples.english: Why won't you even attempt to acknowledge your own mistake?
examples.pronunciation: dou shite jibun no misu o mitomeyou to shinai no ?

examples.japanese: うちの子はいくら言っても勉強しようとしない。
examples.english: No matter what I say to my child, they won't bother to study.
examples.pronunciation: uchi no ko wa ikura ittemo benkyou shiyou to shinai.

id: grammar:n3:ような気がする（ようなきがする）
word: ような気がする（ようなきがする）
meaning: have a feeling that; feels like; seems like; think that ~
related:
tendency/(of personality/appearance/conduct) = seems like:
っぽい
気味
そうに / そうな
みたいだ
ようだ
そうだ
らしい
みたいに
みたいな
ようにみえる
とみえる
がち
に見える
まるで
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（意向形） + ような気がする
connectionSourcePoint: ような気がする（ようなきがする）

examples.japanese: それが本当のような気がする。
examples.english: I feel that it is true.
examples.pronunciation: sore ga hontou no you na ki ga suru.

examples.japanese: 分かるような気がする。
examples.english: I think I understand.
examples.pronunciation: wakaru you na ki ga suru.

examples.japanese: 今日は、宝くじが当るような気がする。
examples.english: I feel like I am going to win the lottery today.
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa takarakuji ga ataru youna ki ga suru.

examples.japanese: この食べ物を毎日食べると、太る気がする。
examples.english: I feel like eating eating this food every day will make me gain weight.
examples.pronunciation: kono tabemono o mainichi taberu to, futoru ki ga suru.

examples.japanese: 卒業したのはつい昨日のような気がする。
examples.english: It seems as if I had graduated only yesterday.
examples.pronunciation: sotsugyou shita no wa tsui kinou no you na ki ga suru.

examples.japanese: その答えは「no」のような気がした。
examples.english: I felt like the answer would have been "no".
examples.pronunciation: sono kotae wa「no」no you na ki ga shita.

examples.japanese: 今朝は散歩したいような気がする。
examples.english: I feel like going for a walk this morning.
examples.pronunciation: kesa wa sanpo shitai you na ki ga suru.

examples.japanese: 最近、私のパソコンが遅くなった気がする。買い換えようかな。
examples.english: Lately I feel like my computer has gotten slower. Maybe I should buy a new one..
examples.pronunciation: saikin, watashi no pasokon ga osokunatta ki ga suru. kaikaeyou kana.

meaning: in order to, so that ~
related: ために
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
goal ように actions to reach goal
unlike ために subject does not have controle over the 'goal'
i.e. he can not make sure, it is not under his full controle to reach the goal and the structure is used to say that the measure undertaken is an attempt to reach the goal
connection:
V（普通形） + ような / ように
Nの + ような / ように
usageJapanese: 具体的な例を挙げる時に使う
usageNotes: 話し言葉では「〜みたいな」を使う

examples.japanese: バスに乗り遅れないように早く出ました。
examples.english: I left early so I would not be late for the bus.
examples.pronunciation: basu ni noriokurenai you ni hayaku demashita.

examples.japanese: 先生の話を忘れないように、メモしておきます。
examples.english: I will take some notes so as to not forget what the teacher said.
examples.pronunciation: sensei no hanashi o wasurenai youni, memo shite okimasu.

examples.japanese: 単語が覚えられるように、フラッシュカードを作りました。
examples.english: I made some flashcards to help me be able to memorize vocabulary.
examples.pronunciation: tango o oboerareru youni, furasshu kaado o tsukuri mashita.

examples.japanese: 皆さんに聞こえるようにもっと大きな声で話してください。
examples.english: Speak with a louder voice so everyone can hear.
examples.pronunciation: mina san ni kikoeru youni motto ookina koe de hanashite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 大学に合格出来るように一生懸命勉強しています。
examples.english: I'm studying my absolute hardest so that I can be accepted to university.
examples.pronunciation: daigaku ni goukaku dekiru youni isshoukenmei benkyou shiteimasu.

examples.japanese: 私は日本の新聞を読めるように、漢字を勉強しています。
examples.english: I'm studying kanji so that I can read Japanese newspapers.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa nihon no shibun o yomeru youni, kanji o benkyou shite imasu.

examples.japanese: 彼女は寝坊しないように、アラームをたくさんかけました。
examples.english: She set a lot of alarms so that she doesn't sleep in.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa nebou shinai youni, araamu o takusan kakemashita.

examples.japanese: いつでも単語が調べられるように、スマホに辞書のアプリをダウンロードしてあります。
examples.english: I downloaded a dictionary application so that I can look up vocabulary words at any time.
examples.pronunciation: istudemo tango ga shirabe rareru youni, sumaho ni jisho no apuri o daunroodo shite arimasu.

examples.japanese: 彼女は勉強中に邪魔されないように、携帯電話の電源を切った。
examples.english: She turned off her cellphone so it wouldn’t bother her while studying.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa benkyouchuu ni jama sarenai you ni, keitai denwa no dengen o kitta.

examples.japanese: 彼は楽しんでいるように見える。

examples.japanese: 都会にいる人は誰でも、いつも活動しているようにみえる。
examples.english: Everyone in the city always seems to be on the go.
examples.pronunciation: tokai ni iru hito wa dare demo, itumo seikatsu shiteiru you ni mieru.

meaning: in comparison to; rather than; more than ~
related:
specific/concrete comparisions (as opposed to figurative comparisons):

に比べて
より

むしろ
というより

・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
量 りょう quantity
used for comparison of actions and thing
A よりも [....] = In comparison to A/ rather than A / More than A [....]

examples.japanese: 兄は私よりも大きい。
examples.english: My older brother is bigger than I am.
examples.pronunciation: ani wa watashi yorimo ookii.

examples.japanese: 量よりも質だ。
examples.english: Quality matters more than quantity.
examples.pronunciation: ryou yori mo shitsu da.

examples.japanese: 私は親よりも早く寝ます。
examples.english: I go to sleep before my parents do.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa oya yorimo hayaku nemasu.

examples.japanese: これまでよりも頑張ります。
examples.english: I'll work even harder than I have been until now.
examples.pronunciation: kore made yorimo ganbarimasu.

examples.japanese: 私は海よりも山の方がいい。
examples.english: I like mountains more than the ocean.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa umi yorimo yama no hou ga ii.

examples.japanese: 誰よりも君のことを愛している。
examples.english: I love you more than anyone else in the world.
examples.pronunciation: dare yorimo kimi no koto o aishiteiru.

examples.japanese: 実際の年齢よりも若く見えます。
examples.english: To look younger than one's actual age.
examples.pronunciation: jissai no nenrei yorimo wakaku miemasu.

examples.japanese: 友情は、他の何よりも大切だ。
examples.english: Friendship is more important than anything else.
examples.pronunciation: yuujou wa, hokano nani yorimo taisetsu da.

meaning: there is no way that; it is impossible to ~
related:
ようがない
impossible to
がたい
わけがない
ようがない
Opposite situation -> Other party thinks Subject is not capeable to do something, and Subject denies it :
ないとはない 
(something) never happens; there is no possibility that
ことはない 
very unlikely to/showing no signs of
そうもない /そうにない
cannot possibly be; hardly とても～ない
thats impossible まさか
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
Shows strong disbelief
This grammar structure modifies a verb, if it is added to a noun or an adjective, the implicit verb is "is/to be = です"
connection:
V(普通形) + わけがない
イA（普通形） + わけがない
ナAな/である + わけがない
Nの/である + わけがない
usageJapanese:
～は考えられない / 絶対に〜でない
話し手が確信を持って、「〜ではない」と言う時に使う表現。可能性を完全に否定する。
usageNotes:
・「〜はずがない」と言い換えが可能。
・「否定形 + わけがない」は二重否定になるので、「必ず〜だ」と、強い確信の込もった推量表現になる。
・会話では「〜わけない」という表現がよく使われる。

examples.japanese: 彼はお金がないですから、家を買えるわけがない。
examples.english: He has no money, there's no way he can buy a house.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa okane ga nai desu kara, ie o kaeru wake ga nai.

examples.japanese: 私がそんなものを食べられるわけがない。
examples.english: There's no way I could eat anything like that..
examples.pronunciation: watashi ga sonna mono o taberareru wake ga nai.

examples.japanese: 彼がそんな早い時間に起きられるわけがない。いつも寝坊しているんだから。
examples.english: There's no way he can get up that early.. He is always sleeping in..
examples.pronunciation: kare ga sonna hayai jikan ni okirareru wake ga nai. itsumo nebou shite irun da kara.

examples.japanese: こんな高いレストランで毎日、食事できるわけがない。
examples.english: There's no way I can eat at such an expensive restaurant every day.
examples.pronunciation: konna takai resutoran de mainichi, shokuji dekiru wake ga nai.

examples.japanese: 全然勉強してないのに、君がJLPT N3に合格するわけがないよ。
examples.english: You haven't been studying at all, there's no way you'll pass the JLPT N3.
examples.pronunciation: zenzen benkyou shite nai noni, kimi ga JLPT N3 ni goukaku suru wake ga nai yo.

examples.japanese: 歌が下手な彼が歌手になれるわけがない。
examples.english: He's no good at singing and there's no way he'll become a singer.
examples.pronunciation: uta ga heta na kare ga kashu ni nareru wake ga nai.

examples.japanese: 嘘ばかりついている彼が、みんなに信用されるわけがないでしょう。
examples.english: There's no way he who is always lying will be trusted by anyone. There's no way he who is always lying will be trusted by everyyone.
examples.pronunciation: uso bakari tsuite iru kare ga, minna ni shinyou sareru wake ga nai deshou.

examples.japanese: 目の悪い彼女にあんな遠くの小さい文字が見えるわけがない。
examples.english: With her bad eyes, there's no way she can read the small text that far away.
examples.pronunciation: me no warui kanojo ni anna tooku no chiisai moji ga mieru wake ga nai.

examples.japanese: こんな安いものがいいもののわけがない。すぐ壊れてしまうだろう。
examples.english: There's no way something this cheap is anything good. It will surely break right away.
examples.pronunciation: konna yasui mono ga ii mono no wake ga nai. sugu kowarete shimau darou.

meaning: for that reason [explanation]; no wonder [explanation]; as you’d expect [explanation]; the fact is [bold speech]; so you mean ~ [paraphrisation]
related:
[paraphirsation] usecase negation -> [wrong paraphrisation]: わけではない
paraphrisation:　ということだ
paraphrisation "in other words", say something in own words, reinterpretation:
わけだ
つまり
すなわち
要するに
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
regarding "so you mean" usecase: --> instead of an paraphrization, if used in a question it enquires the meaning/intention of the other party (female casual speech - Misaka Mikoto) [might omitt the だ]
paraphrisation, asks for agreement (combine with ね) [no wonder]
explanation: [something is in a certain state] + explanation of the cause why it is in that state (explanation is obvious and done with the purpose is to ask for agreement) + わけだ
bold speech: bold speech style +わけだ -> shocking,surprising,assertive statement
connection: 普通形 + わけだ
usageJapanese:
① ・・・なのだから当然～という結果になる。/ だから～になるのか。
ある事実や状況から、当然そうなることを説明したり、説明を聞いてよくわかったときに使う。
② ・・・なので、〜ということだね
相手が言ったことや意見などを反復したり、再度確認するときに使う表現。

examples.japanese: 毎日ギターを弾くから上手に弾けるわけだ。
examples.english: I'm good at playing guitar, because I play every day.
examples.pronunciation: mainichi gitaa o hiku kara jouzu ni hikeru wake da.

examples.japanese: 暑いわけだ。気温が36度もある。
examples.english: No wonder it feels so hot, it's 36 degrees (Celsius) out.
examples.pronunciation: atsui wake da. kion ga 36 do mo aru.

examples.japanese: 「って、知らないわけ？」
examples.english: You mean to say you don't know?
examples.pronunciation: 「tte, shiranai wake?」

examples.japanese: あんなに食べたら太るわけだ。
examples.english: Eating like that it's no wonder they'd gain weight.
examples.pronunciation: annani tabe tara futoru wake da.

examples.japanese: 本当のことを言うと彼はその仕事には向かなかったわけだ。
examples.english: To tell you the truth, he simply was not fit for the job.
examples.pronunciation: hontou no koto o iu to kare wa sono shigoto ni wa mukanakatta wake da.

examples.japanese: 彼女は動物が嫌いだから犬を飼ったことがないわけだ。
examples.english: She doesn't like animals, so there's no way she's ever raised a dog.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa doubutsu ga kirai da kara inu o katta koto ga nai wakeda.

examples.japanese: 彼は日本に10年住んでいるから、日本語が上手なわけだ。
examples.english: He's been living in Japan for 10 years so it's no wonder his Japanese is good.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa nihon ni 10 nen sundeiru kara, nihongo ga jouzu na wake da.

examples.japanese: こんなところで工事をしていたのか。渋滞するわけだ。
examples.english: They are doing construction here, no wonder the roads were crowded..
examples.pronunciation: konna tokoro de kouji o shite ita noka. juutai suru wake da.

examples.japanese: このエアコンは20年間使ったのだ。それは壊れるわけだね。
examples.english: This air conditioner was used for 20 years. It's no wonder it broke.
examples.pronunciation: kono eakon wa 20 nen kan tsukatta no da. sore wa kowareru wake da ne.

meaning: it doesn’t mean that; it is not the case that; I don’t mean that; it is not true that ~
related: opposite([wrong paraphrisation] = [missunderstanding of the other party]) -> ([paraphrisation] = [interpretation of speaker]) わけだ
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
[wrong paraphrisation] it doesnt mean that: other party has interpreted something wrong, and speaker points it out. [(Situation+) Conclusion] + わけではない = Denial of [(Situation+) Conclusion]
connection:
普通形 + わけではない
名詞の接続は「Nなわけではない」と「Nのわけではない」の２通りある。
connectionSourcePoint: わけではない / わけじゃない
usageJapanese:
全部が〜とは言えない。
部分否定の「〜というものではない」と同じ意味で使われる。
また、直前のことから当然予想されることを婉曲的に否定するのにも用いられる。

examples.japanese: 料理が作れないわけではないが、忙しいからあまり作らない。
examples.english: It's not that I can't cook. I'm just busy and don't do it that often.
examples.pronunciation: ryouri ga tsukurenai wake dewa nai ga, isogashii kara amari tsukuranai.

examples.japanese: 先生は何でも知っているわけではありません。
examples.english: It's not like the teacher knows everything.
examples.pronunciation: sensei wa nan demo shitteiru wake dewa arimasen.

examples.japanese: そんなに遠いわけじゃないんですよ。
examples.english: It's not that far away..
examples.pronunciation: sonnani tooi wake janai n desu yo.

examples.japanese: あの人のことが嫌いなわけではないが、一緒にいるとちょっと疲れる。
examples.english: It's not that I hate that person or anything like that. It's just a bit tiring being around them.
examples.pronunciation: ano hito no koto ga kirai na wake dewa nai ga, isshoni iru to chotto tsukareru.

examples.japanese: 親の意見がいつも正しいわけではないが、色々アドバイスはして欲しい。
examples.english: Although it's not like the opinions of parents are always correct, I would like to hear various advice from them.
examples.pronunciation: oya no iken ga itsumo tadashii wake dewa nai ga, iroiro adobaisu wa shite hoshii.

examples.japanese: お金をたくさん持てば幸せになるわけではない。
examples.english: Having a lot of money doesn't mean you will be happy.
examples.pronunciation: okane o takusan moteba shiawase ni naru wake dewa nai.

examples.japanese: この商品を作るのは不可能なわけではないが、時間とコストがかかりますよ。
examples.english: It's not impossible to make this product, but it will take some time and cost a considerable amount of money.
examples.pronunciation: kono shouhin o tsukuru nowa fukanou na wake dewa nai ga, jikan to kosuto ga kakari masu yo.

examples.japanese: 運動をすれば健康になるわけではない。食事や睡眠も大切だ。
examples.english: You won't get healthy just by exercising. Diet and sleep are also essential.
examples.pronunciation: undou o sureba kenkou ni naru wake dewa nai. shokuji ya suimin mo taisetsu da.

meaning: must not; cannot afford to; must, have no choice but to ~
related:
have no choice in something:
てもしょうがない / てもしかたがない
てしょうがない・てしかたがない
ても始まらない
どうしても
しかない
must do something; have to do something:
なければならない
こと
わけにはいかなに
ずにはいられない
ないと
being forced into something against ones wishes: ても始まらない;　てもしょうがない・てもしかたがない;　しかない[and similar]
prohibition:
ちゃいけない
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
used to describe prohibition
can suggest dislike and unwillingness to this prohibition
^this case is used if the verb is casual;non past
in the case that the verb is negative it means: must, have to
connection:
①V（辞書形） + わけにはいかない
②V（ナイ形） + わけにはいかない
usageJapanese:
①～したい気持ちはあるが理由があって～できない
②〜しなければならない / 〜するのが当然だ

examples.japanese: 明日はテストがあるから、ゲームをするわけにはいかない。
examples.english: I have a test tomorrow, so there's no way I can play games right now.
examples.pronunciation: ashita wa tesuto ga aru kara, geemu o suru wake niwa ikanai.

examples.japanese: 日本の税金は、高くても払わないわけにはいかない。
examples.english: Even if Japanese taxes are high, they must be paid. Even if Japanese taxes are high, one cannot afford to not pay them
examples.pronunciation: nihon no zeikin wa, takaku temo harawanai wake niwa ikanai.

examples.japanese: あとで車を運転するので、今お酒を飲むわけにはいかない。
examples.english: I will be driving a car later, so I cannot drink any alcohol now.
examples.pronunciation: ato de kuruma o unten suru node, ima osake o nomu wake niwa ikanai.

examples.japanese: 行くと約束したから、行かないわけにはいきません。
examples.english: I promised that I’d go, so I have no choice but to go.
examples.pronunciation: iku to yakusoku shita kara, ikanai wake ni wa ikimasen.

examples.japanese: あんなに面白そうな映画を、見に行かないわけにはいかない。
examples.english: That movie looks really interesting, I absolutely need to go and see it!
examples.pronunciation: anna ni omoshiro sou na eiga o, mi ni ikanai wake niwa ikanai.

examples.japanese: 今日は彼女の誕生日だけど、システムトラブルでみんな頑張っているし、私だけ帰るわけにはいかない。
examples.english: Today is my girlfriend's birthday, but there is some system trouble at work and everyone is working hard to fix it so it's not like only I can go home..
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa kanojo no tanjoubi da kedo, shisutemutoraburu de minna ganbatte iru shi, watashi dake kaeru wake niwa ikanai.

examples.japanese: ちょっと熱があるけど、今日は大事な会議があるから休むわけにはいかない。
examples.english: I have a little fever, but today there is an important meeting so there's no way I can take the day off.
examples.pronunciation: chotto netsu ga aru kedo, kyou wa daiji na kaigi ga aru kara yasumu wake niwa ikanai.

examples.japanese: 隣の部屋で今、赤ちゃんが寝ているので、ピアノを弾くわけにはいかない。
examples.english: The baby is sleeping next door right now, so I can't play piano.
examples.pronunciation: tonari no heya de iam, akachan ga nete iru node, piano o hiku wake niwa ikanai.

examples.japanese: せっかく東京へ来たのだから、東京タワーに行かないわけにはいかない。
examples.english: Since I've come all the way to Tokyo, I must go to see Tokyo Tower!
examples.pronunciation: sekkaku toukyou e kita no dakara, toukyou tawaa ni ikanai wake niwa ikanai.

meaning: on purpose; intentionally ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・action
in this sentece action is implied therefore nothing is marked
used to describe actions that suggest a hidden intent
connection: わざと + verb phrase

examples.japanese: わざとの可能性がある。
examples.english: There is the possibility that it was deliberate.
examples.pronunciation: wazato no kanousei ga aru.

examples.japanese: 知っていながら、わざと知らないふりをする。
examples.english: To pretend not to know something even though one knows it.
examples.pronunciation: shittei nagara, wazato shiranai furi o suru.

meaning: on purpose; intentionally ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・action
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
used to describe actions that suggest a hidden intent
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: わざと op i

examples.japanese: 彼はわざと間違えた。
examples.english: He made a mistake on purpose.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa wazato machigaeta.

examples.japanese: 私は彼がわざとそうしたとは思いません。
examples.english: I don't think that he did that on purpose.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa kare ga wazato sou shita to wa omoimasen.

examples.japanese: それをわざと壊したのではありません。
examples.english: It's not like I broke that on purpose.
examples.pronunciation: sore o wazato kowashita no dewa arimasen.

examples.japanese: 彼はわざとその質問に答えなかった。
examples.english: He didn't answer the question on purpose.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa wazato sono shitsumon ni kotaenakatta.

examples.japanese: 彼女がわざと遅れて来たのはほぼ確かだと私は思っている。
examples.english: I'm pretty sure she came late on purpose.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo ga wazato okurete kita no wa hobo tashika da to omotteiru.

meaning: to go to the trouble of; to go out of one's way to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: わざわざ

examples.japanese: 私が困っているとき、彼女はわざわざ助けてくれた。
examples.english: She went out of her way to help me when I was in trouble.
examples.pronunciation: watashi ga komatte iru toki, kanojo wa wazawaza tasukete kureta.

examples.japanese: あなたはわざわざ私の為にそれを買ってくれたのですか？
examples.english: Did you buy that just for me?
examples.pronunciation: anata wa wazawaza watashi no tame ni sore o katte kureta no desu ka?

examples.japanese: わざわざ遠い所から会いにきてくれてありがとう。
examples.english: Thanks for going out of your way to see me from so far away.
examples.pronunciation: wazawaza tooi tokoro kara ai ni kite kurete arigatou.

examples.japanese: 彼は忙しいだが、わざわざ市内の観光を案内してくれた。
examples.english: He is very busy, but he took some time to show me around the tourist spots in the city.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa isogashii da ga, wazawaza shinai no kankou o annai shite kureta.

examples.japanese: 友達は恋人に会いたいから、わざわざニューヨークへ行った。
examples.english: My friend really wanted to see his girlfriend, so he went all the way to New York.
examples.pronunciation: tomodachi wa koibito ni aitai kara, wazawaza nyuuyooku e itta.

examples.japanese: わざわざ駅まで送ってくれなくてもいいよ。
examples.english: You don't need to trouble yourself to see me off to the station.
examples.pronunciation: wazawaza eki made okutte kurena kutemo ii yo.

examples.japanese: 彼があんな遠くからわざわざ訪ねてきてくれて本当に嬉しかった。
examples.english: I was really glad he came all the way here to visit from such a far distance.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga anna tooku kara wazawaza tazunete kite kurete hontou ni ureshikatta.

meaning: for example; starting with
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
from an array of representatives one is chosen, usually the most prominent
connection: N + をはじめ
usageJapanese:
〜を第一に
代表的なものを１つ「〜をはじめ」でピックアップし、それから、他の同じグループのものを言うときに使う。

examples.japanese: 東京には東京タワーをはじめ多くの観光地がある。
examples.english: In Tokyo, there are many tourist attractions like Tokyo Tower and so on.
examples.pronunciation: toukyou ni wa toukyou tawaa o hajime ooku no kankouchi ga aru.

examples.japanese: 日本のアニメはドラゴンボールをはじめ、面白いものが多い。
examples.english: There are many interesting Japanese anime, like Dragon Ball and so on.
examples.pronunciation: nihon no anime wa doragon booru o hajime, omoshiroi mono ga ooi.

examples.japanese: そのポスターには私をはじめ何人かがサインしていた。
examples.english: Myself and numerous other people signed that poster.
examples.pronunciation: sono posutaa ni wa watashi o hajime nanninka ga sain shite ita.

examples.japanese: 日本の春は、お花見をはじめたくさんのイベントがある。
examples.english: In spring in Japan, there is cherry blossom viewing and many other events.
examples.pronunciation: nihon no haru wa, ohanami o hajime takusan no ibento ga aru.

examples.japanese: この本には、犬をはじめ、色々な動物のことが書いてあります。
examples.english: In this book, a lot of things are written about dogs and various other animals.
examples.pronunciation: kono hon ni wa, inu o hajime, iroirona doubutsu no koto ga kaite arimasu.

examples.japanese: 会議には中国をはじめ、アジアの国々が参加した。
examples.english: China and other Asian countries participated in the conference.
examples.pronunciation: kaigi wa chuugoku o hajime, ajia no kuniguni ga sanka shita.

examples.japanese: 私には両親をはじめ、男の兄弟も女の姉弟もいないの。
examples.english: I have no parents, brothers or sisters.
examples.pronunciation: watashi ni wa ryoushin o hajime, otoko no kyoudai mo onna no kyoudai mo inai no.

examples.japanese: この野菜には、ビタミンＣをはじめ、たくさんの栄養素が含まれています。
examples.english: This vegetable has a lot of nutrients inside of it like vitamin C.
examples.pronunciation: kono yasai ni wa, bitamin c o hajime, takusan no eiyouso ga fukumarete imasu.

examples.japanese: 東京をはじめ、全国の主要都市で新製品の発売が決まった。
examples.english: It’s been decided that the new product will be released in Tokyo and other major cities.
examples.pronunciation: toukyou o hajime, zenkoku no shuyoutoshi de shinseihin no hatsubai ga kimatta.

id: grammar:n3:を中心に（をちゅうしんに）
word: を中心に（をちゅうしんに）
meaning: focused on; centered on
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
often is used to provide details for another noun [which comes after を中心に]
connection: N + を中心に / を中心において
usageJapanese: 〜を基点にして / 〜を集中的に

examples.japanese: 文法を中心に、日本語を勉強する。
examples.english: To study Japanese with a focus on grammar.
examples.pronunciation: bunpou o chuushin ni, nihongo o benkyou suru.

examples.japanese: 私の人生はすべて、家族を中心に回っている。
examples.english: Everything in my life revolves around family.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no jinsei wa subete, kazoku o chuushin ni mawatteiru.

examples.japanese: その物語は彼女の一生を中心にして書かれている。
examples.english: That story is written around her life story.
examples.pronunciation: sono monogatari wa kanojo no isshou o chuushin ni shite kakareteiru.

examples.japanese: 午前中は文法を勉強します。午後は会話を中心に勉強することになっています。
examples.english: We’ll study grammar in the morning. In the afternoon, we’ll focus on learning speaking skills.
examples.pronunciation: gozenchuu wa bunpou o benkyou shimasu. gogo wa kaiwa o chuushin ni benkyou suru koto ni natte imasu.

examples.japanese: この作家の作品は、若い女性を中心に読まれている。
examples.english: This author’s books are mostly read by young women.
examples.pronunciation: kono sakka no sakuhin wa, wakai josei o chuushin ni yomarete iru.

examples.japanese: この店はスキー用品を中心としたスポーツ専門店です。
examples.english: This sport store specializes in ski equipment.
examples.pronunciation: kono mise wa sukii youhin o chuushin toshita supootsu senmonten desu.

examples.japanese: 地球は太陽を中心として回っている。
examples.english: The Earth is revolving around the Sun.
examples.pronunciation: chikyuu wa taiyou o chuushin toshite mawatte iru.

examples.japanese: 日本は東京を中心に経済が発展している。
examples.english: The Japanese economy is expanding with Tokyo as its center.
examples.pronunciation: nihon wa toukyou o chuushin ni keizai ga hatten shite iru.

meaning: filled with; full of
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
noun is often an emotion
connection: N + を込めて
usageJapanese:
〜を入れて / 〜と一緒に
気持ちや力などを入れて何かをするという意味
usageNotes:
[一緒によく使われる言葉]
愛、気持ち、心、祈り、恨み、力、願い

examples.japanese: 彼女に愛を込めて手紙を書きました。
examples.english: I wrote her a letter full of my love.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo ni ai o komete tegami o kakimashita.

examples.japanese: 平和への願いを込めて、この歌を作りました。
examples.english: I wrote this song filled with my wish for peace.
examples.pronunciation: heiwa e no negai o komete, kono uta o tsukuri mashita.

examples.japanese: 私は夫のために毎日愛情を込めてお弁当を作っています。
examples.english: To help support my husband, every day I make him a lunch box full of my love.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa otto no tame ni mainichi aijou o komete obentou o tsukutte imasu.

examples.japanese: 彼女は故郷への思いを込めて、その曲を作ったそうだ。
examples.english: She apparently made that song full of thoughts of her hometown.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa furusato e no omoi o komete, sono kyoku o tsukutta sou da.

examples.japanese: 父の日には、いつも頑張っている父に感謝の気持ちを込めてプレゼントを贈りました。
examples.english: On father's day, I sent a present full of my gratitude to my always hard-working father.
examples.pronunciation: chichi no hi niwa, itsumo ganbatte iru chichi ni kansha no kimochi o komete purezento o okuri mashita.

examples.japanese: 大学受験の日、息子が合格しますようにの願いを込めてお弁当を作った。
examples.english: On the day of the university entrance exam for my son, I made him a lunch box full of my wishes for him to pass.
examples.pronunciation: daigaku juken no hi, musuko ga goukaku shimasu youni no negai o komete obentou o tsukutta.

examples.japanese: 当店の製品は全て職人が心を込めて手作業で作っています。
examples.english: At this shop, all of our products are made by hand and full of heart by our expert craftsmen.
examples.pronunciation: touten no seihin wa subete shokunin ga kokoro o komete tesagyou de tsukutte imasu.

examples.japanese: 日本人は親しみの気持ちを込めて「本屋さん」や「お寿司屋さん」のようにお店に「さん」を付けることがある。
examples.english: Japanese people sometimes attach the suffix "san" to a shop like "bookstore" or "sushi shop" with a feeling of familiarity.
examples.pronunciation: nihonjin wa shitashimi no kimochi o komete honyasan ya osushiyasan no youni omise ni san o tsukeru koto ga aru.

id: grammar:n3:を通じて / を通して（をつうじて / をとおして）
word: を通じて / を通して（をつうじて / をとおして）
meaning: through; via; throughout; by ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
noun is usually something one could describe as an interval [something that has a time, or a space]
or which can be used as a medium or tool
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: を通じて
Fixed expression / pattern: を通して（をつうじて
Fixed expression / pattern: をとおして）
connectionSourcePoint: を通じて / を通して（をつうじて / をとおして）

examples.japanese: そのことは新聞を通じて知った。
examples.english: I learned that from a newspaper.
examples.pronunciation: sono koto wa shinbun o tsuujite shitta.

examples.japanese: この駅前広場では年間を通じて様々なイベントが催される。
examples.english: At the public square in front of this station, throughout the year there are various events held.
examples.pronunciation: kono ekimae hiroba de wa nenkan o tsuujite samazamana ibento ga moyoosareru.

examples.japanese: 二人は大学のサークル活動を通して知り合った。
examples.english: These 2 met through their university club activities.
examples.pronunciation: futari wa daigaku no saakuru katsudou o tooshite shiriatta.

examples.japanese: 旅を通じていろいろな経験をし、それをこれからの仕事に生かしたい。
examples.english: I've gained various experience in my travels, and want to capitalize on that with my work from now on. Through my travels, I've gained various experience, and want to capitalize on that with my work from now on.
examples.pronunciation: tabi o tsuujite iroirona keiken o shi, sore o kore kara no shigoto ni ikashitai.

examples.japanese: 後ろのほうの人にも聞こえるよう、マイクを通して話した。
examples.english: So the people in the back could also hear, I spoke through a microphone.
examples.pronunciation: ushiro no hou no hito ni mo kikoeru you, maiku o tooshite hanashita.

examples.japanese: 私たちは本や授業を通じて、様々な知識を得ている。
examples.english: We are gaining various information through books and class.
examples.pronunciation: watashitachi wa hon ya jugyou o tsuujite, samazama na chishiki o eteiru.

examples.japanese: 大学の四年間を通して、経営とは何かということを学んだ。
examples.english: Through my 4 years at university I learned about what is management.
examples.pronunciation: daigaku no yonnenkan o tooshite, keiei to wa nanika to iu koto o mananda.

examples.japanese: 京都は代表的な観光地で、年間を通して観光者が多い。
examples.english: Kyoto is an exemplary tourist destination and throughout the year there are many tourists.
examples.pronunciation: kyouto wa daihyoutekina kankouchi de, nenkan o tooshite kankousha ga ooi.

meaning: I hear that; heard that ~
related: そうだ
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
範囲 はんい scope
教科書 きょうかしょ textbook
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: んだって

examples.japanese: 彼、彼女ができたんだって。
examples.english: I heard he got a girlfriend.
examples.pronunciation: kare, kanojo ga dekita ndatte.

examples.japanese: 知らないんだって。
examples.english: They said that they did not know.
examples.pronunciation: shiranai nda tte.

examples.japanese: クリスさんがJLPTのN2合格したんだって。
examples.english: I heard that Chris passed the JLPT N2!
examples.pronunciation: kurisu san ga JLPT no N2 goukaku shita nda tte.

examples.japanese: 妹が結婚しているんだって。
examples.english: I heard that my sister is going to get married.
examples.pronunciation: imouto ga kekkon shiteiru ndatte.

examples.japanese: この店のカレー、美味しいんだって。
examples.english: I heard that this restaurant has good curry.
examples.pronunciation: kono mise no karee, oishii ndatte.

examples.japanese: 聞いた？トムさんは卒業したら国に帰るんだって。
examples.english: Did you hear? Tom is heading back to his home country after graduation.
examples.pronunciation: kiita? tomu san wa sotsugyou shitara kuni ni kaeru datte.

examples.japanese: 明日はめちゃくちゃ寒くなるんだって。出かける時に上着を着てね。
examples.english: I heard tomorrow is going to get much worse. Make sure to to wear a jacket when you go out.
examples.pronunciation: ashita wa mechakucha samuku narun datte. dekakeru toki ni uwagi o kite ne.

examples.japanese: あの二人は来月結婚するだって。付き合うのは1ヶ月にも経ってないのに、早いですね！
examples.english: I hear those 2 are getting married next month. They've only been dating for 1 month, that's really fast!
examples.pronunciation: ano futari wa raigetsu kekkon suru datte. tsukiau nowa ikkagetsu nimo tattenai, hayai desu ne!

examples.japanese: 試験の範囲は、教科書の100ページから150ページまでなんですって。
examples.english: I heard that the test will cover from page 100 until page 150.
examples.pronunciation: shiken no hani wa, kyoukasho no hyaku peeji kara hyaku go juu peeji made nan desu tte.

meaning: emphasis; what on earth; what in the world
related:
emphasis:
こそ
一体
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
conveys disbelief and surprise of subject
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 一体

examples.japanese: 一体どこで彼に会ったんだ。
examples.english: Where on earth did you meet him?
examples.pronunciation: ittai doko de kare ni atta nda.

examples.japanese: あなたは一体何をしていますか？
examples.english: What exactly are you doing?
examples.pronunciation: anata wa ittai nani o shite imasu ka?

examples.japanese: あの人は一体何の者だ？
examples.english: Who in the world is that?
examples.pronunciation: ano hito wa ittai nan no mono da?

examples.japanese: 彼女は一体どこに住んでいるだろう。
examples.english: Where in the world does she live?
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa ittai doko ni sunde iru darou.

examples.japanese: 一体誰が彼女にそんなことを言ったのか？
examples.english: Who could have said such a thing to her?
examples.pronunciation: ittai dare ga kanojo ni sonna koto o itta no ka?

examples.japanese: この騒ぎは一体なんですか?
examples.english: What is this fuss all about?
examples.pronunciation: kono sawagi wa ittai nan desu ka?

examples.japanese: 君が欲しいのは一体何だ。
examples.english: What exactly is it that you want?
examples.pronunciation: kimi ga hoshii no wa ittai nan da?

examples.japanese: 一体彼の話は本当だろうか。
examples.english: Could his story possibly be true?
examples.pronunciation: ittai kare no hanashi wa hontou darou ka?

meaning: all at once
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj ・phrase
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 一度に

examples.japanese: 彼は一度にクッキーを3つ取った。
examples.english: He took 3 cookies at once.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa ichido ni kukkii o mitsu totta.

examples.japanese: 一度に３冊の本を借りることが出来ます。
examples.english: You can borrow three books at a time.
examples.pronunciation: ichido ni 3 satsu no hon o kariru koto ga dekimasu.

examples.japanese: 一度に全部はできないよ。
examples.english: You can't do everything at once.
examples.pronunciation: ichido ni zenbu wa dekinai yo.

examples.japanese: 彼は一度に６個の箱を運んだ。
examples.english: He carried six boxes at once.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa ichido ni rokko no hako o hakonda.

examples.japanese: その薬品は一度に全部使用しなければなりません。
examples.english: You must use all of that medicine at the same time.
examples.pronunciation: sono yakuhin wa ichido ni zenbu shiyou shinakereba narimasen.

examples.japanese: 私はそれらを一度にまとめて注文したい。
examples.english: I want to order those all together.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa sorera o ichido ni matomete chuumon shitai.

examples.japanese: 一度に二つの計画を試してはいけない。
examples.english: You should't attempt two projects at the same time.
examples.pronunciation: ichido ni futatsu no keikaku o tameshite wa ikenai.

examples.japanese: このエレベーターは一度に１０人くらい運べる。
examples.english: This elevator can carry about 10 people at once.
examples.pronunciation: kono erebeetaa wa ichido ni 10 nin kurai hakoberu.

meaning: more and more; continue to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
acceleration/continuation of (implied development) of verb
connection: N / な-adj + 一方だ

examples.japanese: 最近、この町の人口は減る一方だ。
examples.english: This town's population has been decreasing lately.
examples.pronunciation: saikin, kono machi no jinkou wa heru ippou da.

examples.japanese: 日本に来る外国人の数は増える一方だ。
examples.english: The number of foreigners coming to Japan continues to increase.
examples.pronunciation: nihon ni kuru gaikokujin no kazu wa fueru ippou da.

examples.japanese: 交通事故は増える一方だ。
examples.english: Traffic accidents have been on the rise.
examples.pronunciation: koutsuu jiko wa fueru ippou da.

examples.japanese: 私は全然勉強していないので、成績は下がる一方だ。
examples.english: I never study and so my grades just keep dropping.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa zenzen benkyou shite inai node, seiseki wa sagaru ippou da.

examples.japanese: 日本の人口はこのままだと減少する一方だと思います。
examples.english: With Japan's population situation as it is, I think it will just continue to decline.
examples.pronunciation: nihon no jinkou wa kono mama da to genshou suru ippou da to omoimasu.

examples.japanese: 広告を出しているのに、お客さんは減る一方だ。
examples.english: Despite advertising, the number of customers is decreasing.
examples.pronunciation: koukoku o dashite iru no ni, o kyaku san wa heru ippou da.

examples.japanese: 増税により、生活は苦しくなる一方だ。
examples.english: Tax increases are making life harder.
examples.pronunciation: zouzei ni yori, seikatsu wa kurushiku naru ippou da.

examples.japanese: 地球環境は、年々悪くなる一方だ。
examples.english: The global environment is getting worse year after year. The global environment continues to grow worse year after year
examples.pronunciation: chikyuu kankyou wa, nennen warukunaru ippou da.

examples.japanese: 医者に出された薬を飲んでいるのに、よくなるどころか、症状はひどくなる一方だよ。
examples.english: Even though I'm taking the medicine my doctor gave me, I am not getting better. On the contrary, my symptoms keep getting worse.
examples.pronunciation: isha ni dasareta kusuri o nondeiru noni, yoku naru dokoro ka, shoujou ga hidoku naru ippou da yo.

meaning: to finish doing ~
related:
finish an action/do to the end/do completely
切る
て済む
通す
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
会社 かいしゃ company; corporation
製品 せいひん manufactured goods; finished goods; product
意見 いけん opinion; view; comment
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 上げる（あげる)

examples.japanese: 研究のレポートを一日で書き上げた。
examples.english: I wrote my research report in one day. I finished writing my research report in one day
examples.pronunciation: kenkyuu no repooto o ichinichi de kaki ageta.

meaning: to finish doing ~
related:
finish an action/do to the end/do completely
切る
て済む
通す
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
会社 かいしゃ company; corporation
製品 せいひん manufactured goods; finished goods; product
意見 いけん opinion; view; comment
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 上げる

examples.japanese: 彼女は小説を書き上げた。
examples.english: She finished writing her novel.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa shousetsu o kaki ageta.

examples.japanese: この会社は新製品を作り上げた。
examples.english: This company has created a new product.
examples.pronunciation: kono kaisha wa shinseihin o tsukuri ageta.

examples.japanese: みんなの意見を取り入れて、とてもいいプランが出来上がった。
examples.english: A very good plan was completed, incorporating the opinions of everyone
examples.pronunciation: minna no iken o tori irete, totemo ii puran ga deki agatta.

examples.japanese: 出来上がった料理を、お客さんのところに運ぶのが私の仕事です。
examples.english: It is my job to bring the finished food to the customer.
examples.pronunciation: deki agatta ryouri o, okyaku san no tokoro ni hakobu noga watashi no shigoto desu.

examples.japanese: 注文していた年賀状が刷り上がった。
examples.english: The new year's card I ordered was printed.
examples.pronunciation: chuumon shite ita nengajou ga suri agatta

examples.japanese: この粉を練り上げると、おいしい和菓子になります。
examples.english: When this powder is kneaded, it becomes a delicious Japanese sweet.
examples.pronunciation: kono kona o neri ageru to, oishii wagashi ni narimasu.

examples.japanese: スパゲッティがゆで上がったら、素早くソースを絡めます。
examples.english: Once the spaghetti has been boiled, quickly mix the sauce.
examples.pronunciation: supagetti ga yude agattara, subayaku soosu o karame masu.

meaning: upon; after; when; for; in order to
related:
first do X then do Y:
てからでないと
てから
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
marks necessary condition to achieve sometimg
'necessary condition' + うえで + 'achievement' 
てから
connection:
V（タ形） + 上で
N + の上で
usageJapanese:
まず〜してから。
単に「前件が起こる→後件が起こる」ではなく、前件は後件が起こるための前提条件であることを表す。
usageNotes: 接続する動詞は意志動詞のみ

examples.japanese: 外国語を勉強する上で辞書はなくてはならないものだ。
examples.english: In order to study a foreign language, you need to have a dictionary.
examples.pronunciation: gaikokugo o benkyousuru uede jisho wa nakutewa naranai monoda.

examples.japanese: 仕事をする上で大切なのは、周りとのコミュニケーションです。
examples.english: In order to do one's job, what's important is communication with those around you.
examples.pronunciation: shigoto o suru ue de taisetsu na no wa, mawari to no komyunikeeshon desu.

examples.japanese: この薬は説明書をよく読んだ上で、ご使用ください。
examples.english: Please use this medicine after reading the instructions carefully.
examples.pronunciation: kono kusuri wa setsumeisho o yoku yonda ue de, go shiyou kudasai.

examples.japanese: 実物を見た上で買うかどうか決めるつもりだ。
examples.english: Once I see the real thing in person, I will decide whether to buy or not.
examples.pronunciation: jitsubutsu o mita uede kauka douka kimeru tsumorida.

examples.japanese: 留学の件は家族と相談した上でご返事いたします。
examples.english: Regarding the study abroad, once you consult with your family I will give my answer.
examples.pronunciation: ryuugaku no ken wa kazoku to soudan shita ue de go henji itashimasu.

examples.japanese: 工事計画は、周辺住民との話し合いの上で決められるべきだ。
examples.english: The construction plan should be decided after discussing with the local residents.
examples.pronunciation: kouji keikaku wa, shuuhen juumin to no hanashiai no ue de kimerareru beki da.

examples.japanese: 契約の内容をご確認の上で、サインをお願いいたします。
examples.english: Please sign once you confirm the contents of the contract.
examples.pronunciation: keiyaku no naiyou o go kakunin no ue de, sain o onegai itashimasu.

examples.japanese: 予約の時間を確認の上で、ご来店ください。
examples.english: Please visit us after confirming the reservation time.
examples.pronunciation: yoyaku no jikan o kakunin no ue de, go raiten kudasai.

examples.japanese: この書類に署名の上、窓口に提出してください。
examples.english: Please sign these papers and bring them to the counter.
examples.pronunciation: kono shorui ni shomei no ue, madoguchi ni teishutsu shite kudasai.

meaning: as well; besides; in addition to; not only… but also ~
related:
listing of attributes:
だけでなく
はもちろん
ばかりでなく
も
も～ば～も
とか～とか
し
さらに
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
曇る = くもる = to cloud up, to fog up, to mist up, to become dim
grammar structure used to create a list of 2 qualities:
first quality + 上に + second quality
connection: N / phrase / sentence + 上に

examples.japanese: 今日は曇っている上に風が強いので、とても寒く感じられる。
examples.english: Today in addition to being cloudy, the wind is strong and so it feels very cold.
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa kumotteiru ue ni kaze ga tsuyoi node, totemo samuku kanjirareru.

examples.japanese: この店の料理はおいしい。その上、値段も安いのでいつもお客さんが多い。
examples.english: This restaurant's food is delicious. In addition, the prices are cheap so there are always a lot of customers.
examples.pronunciation: kono mise no ryouri wa oishii. sono ue, nedan mo yasui no de itsumo okyakusan ga ooi.

examples.japanese: このパソコンは安い上に、機能も多いので、最近とても売れている。
examples.english: This computer is not only cheap, but it has a lot of functionality, so lately it has been selling very well.
examples.pronunciation: kono pasokon wa yasui ue ni, kinou mo ooi no de, saikin totemo urete iru.

examples.japanese: この手続きは、面倒な上に時間もかかるので、皆がいやがっている。
examples.english: This procedure is troublesome and time consuming so everyone hates it.
examples.pronunciation: kono tetsuduki wa, mendou na ue ni jikan mo kakaru no de, minaga iyagatte iru.

examples.japanese: この町は、地下鉄が通った上に、デパートもできて、便利になった。
examples.english: This town has become convenient now is connected by subway and in addition has built some department stores.
examples.pronunciation: kono machi wa, chikatetsu ga tootta ue ni, depaato mo dekite, benri ni natta.

examples.japanese: この仕事は危険な上に、給料も低いし、最悪だ。
examples.english: This job is not only dangerous, but the salary is low, so it's pretty much the worst.
examples.pronunciation: kono shigoto wa kiken na ue ni, kyuuryou mo hikui shi, saiaku da.

examples.japanese: この本は漢字が多い上に、字が小さいので子どもには読みにくいです。
examples.english: This book has a lot of kanji, and to top things off the characters are small so it is difficult for children to read.
examples.pronunciation: kono hon wa kanji ga ooi ue ni, ji ga chiisai no de kodomo ni wa yominikui desu.

examples.japanese: あの建物のトイレは汚い上に、いつもトイレットペーパーがないので、あまり行きたくない。
examples.english: That building not only has a dirty bathroom, but there is never any toilet paper so I don't really want to go there.
examples.pronunciation: ano tatemono no toire wa kitanai ue ni, itsumo toiretto peepaa ga nai no de, amari ikitakunai.

meaning: currently; during; at some point; throughout; before the end of ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
can either describe a point in time or (part of) an time-interval
or alternatively either a point in space or an area
in case of interval or area always the entriety
always relative to a noun that describes something that has a (time)duration or space
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 中

examples.japanese: 午前中がいちばん調子がいいです。
examples.english: During the morning is the best.
examples.pronunciation: gozen chuu ga ichiban choushi ga ii desu.

examples.japanese: 英語は世界中で話されています。
examples.english: English is spoken throughout the world.
examples.pronunciation: eigo wa sekaijuu de hanasarete imasu.

examples.japanese: この事件に関しては、ただいま確認中でございます。
examples.english: We are currently checking this case.
examples.pronunciation: kono jiken ni kanshite wa, tadaima kakuninchuu degozaimasu.

examples.japanese: 私は一晩中、両親の夢をみていた。
examples.english: I dreamed of my parents all through the night.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa hitobanjuu, ryoushin no yume o mite ita.

examples.japanese: 入院中、友達が書いてくれた授業のノートを頼りに勉強した。
examples.english: During my hospital stay, I studied with my class notes written by my friends.
examples.pronunciation: nyuuinchuu, tomodachi ga kaite kureta jugyou no no-to wo tayori ni benkyou shita.

meaning: for example; for instance ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 例えば

examples.japanese: 日本の食べ物は美味しいです。例えば、すしはすごく美味しい！
examples.english: Japanese food is delicious, for example, sushi is so delicious!
examples.pronunciation: nihon no tabemono wa oishii desu. tatoeba, sushi wa sugoku oishii!

examples.japanese: 私にはたくさん趣味がある。例えば魚釣り、登山です。
examples.english: I have many hobbies, fishing and climbing for example.
examples.pronunciation: watashi ni wa takusan shumi ga aru. tatoeba sakana tsuri, tozan desu.

examples.japanese: 私は例えばイタリアやスペインなど外国へ行きたい。
examples.english: I want to go abroad, for instance, to Italy and Spain.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa tatoeba itaria ya supein nado gaikoku e ikitai.

examples.japanese: 外国、例えばアメリカへ行ったことがありますか。
examples.english: Have you visited any foreign countries, say America?
examples.pronunciation: gaikoku, tatoeba amerika e itta koto ga arimasu ka?

examples.japanese: 彼は不思議な生物、例えばヘビのような生物が好きです。
examples.english: He likes strange animals, things like snakes, for example.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa fushigina seibutsu, tatoeba hebi no you na seibutsu ga suki desu.

examples.japanese: 私はうるさい音楽は好きではない。例えばロックのような。
examples.english: I don't like noisy music, such as rock.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa urusai ongaku wa suki dewa nai. tatoeba rokku no youna.

examples.japanese: 日本ではきれいな都市がたくさんあります。例えば京都、奈良だ。
examples.english: Japan is full of beautiful cities. Kyoto and Nara, for example.
examples.pronunciation: nihon dewa kirei na toshi ga takusan arimasu. tatoeba kyouto, nara da.

examples.japanese: 彼の部屋に色んなものがある。例えば、ゲーム機、ジムの物など。
examples.english: There are various things in his room. For example, game consoles, gym items, etc.
examples.pronunciation: kare no heya ni iron na mono ga aru. tatoeba, geemuki, jimu no mono nado.

meaning: to do something completely to the end
related:
finish an action/do to the end/do completely
通す
て済む
上げる
opposite grammar: unable to do; too much to finish/complete 切れない
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 切る

examples.japanese: どうしてそう言い切れるのですか。
examples.english: How can you say that?
examples.pronunciation: doushite sou ii kireru no desu ka?

examples.japanese: 長い小説を2日間で読み切った。
examples.english: I finished reading the long novel in two days.
examples.pronunciation: nagai shousetsu o futsukakan de yomikitta.

examples.japanese: お小遣いを使い切ってしまった。
examples.english: I spent all my pocket money.
examples.pronunciation: okozukai o tsukaikitte shimatta.

examples.japanese: 今日はもう魚を全部売り切れた。
examples.english: I've already sold all of the fish for today.
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa mou sakana o zenbu uri kireta.

examples.japanese: どんなに食べ物が多くても食べ切れるぐらいお腹すいた。
examples.english: I was so hungry that I could eat all the food, regardless of how much there may be.
examples.pronunciation: donnani tabemono ga ookutemo tabe kireru gurai onaka suita.

examples.japanese: 10キロを走り切るのはすごく大変なことだ。
examples.english: It's pretty tough to finish running 10 kilometers.
examples.pronunciation: juu kiro o hashiri kiru no wa sugoku taihen na koto da.

examples.japanese: たくさんあった宿題のレポートを書き切りました。
examples.english: I finished writing all of numerous reports I had for homework.
examples.pronunciation: takusan atta shuukidai no repooto o kaki kirimashita.

examples.japanese: どんなことでも、全力でやり切ったあとは、気分がいい。
examples.english: No matter what task it may be, it always feel good after you finish giving it your all.
examples.pronunciation: donna koto demo, zenryoku de yari kitta ato wa, kibun ga ii.

meaning: unable to do; too much to finish/complete
related: opposite grammar: to finish/to completely do: 切る
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 切れない

examples.japanese: この部屋に100人は入り切れない。
examples.english: This room cannot fit 100 people.
examples.pronunciation: kono heya ni 100 nin wa hairi kirenai.

examples.japanese: 彼は使い切れないほど金がある。
examples.english: He has more money than he can spend.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa tsukai kirenai hodo kane ga aru.

examples.japanese: このお酒は強すぎて、飲み切れなかった。
examples.english: This liquor is too strong, I couldn't finish drinking it all.
examples.pronunciation: kono osake wa tsuyo sugite, nomi kirena katta.

examples.japanese: いっぱい注文しないで、二人で食べ切れないから。
examples.english: Don't order so much, we won't be able to eat it all with just the 2 of us.
examples.pronunciation: ippai chuumon shinai de, futari de tabe kirenai kara.

examples.japanese: 明日のテストまでには、全ての文法は勉強し切れないな。
examples.english: I won't be able to finish studying all of the grammar before tomorrow's test.
examples.pronunciation: ashita no tesuto made niwa, subete no bunpou wa benkyou shi kirenai na.

examples.japanese: ご飯の量が多くて食べ切れないよ。
examples.english: I can’t finish the rice because there is too much.
examples.pronunciation: gohan no ryou ga ookute tabe kirenai yo.

examples.japanese: 数え切れないほどたくさん星が光っている。
examples.english: An uncountable number of stars are shinning (in the sky).
examples.pronunciation: kazoe kirenai hodo takusan hoshi ga hikatteiru.

examples.japanese: うちのトイレットペーパーは、引っ越しまでに使い切れないから、お隣さんにあげようか。
examples.english: We won't be able to finish using all of this toilet paper before we move, so why don't we give it to one of our neighbors?
examples.pronunciation: uchi no toiretto peepaa wa, hikkoshi made ni tsukai kirenai kara, otonari san ni ageyou ka.

meaning: not really, not particularly
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
when used with a noun or adjective -> denial of implied state(of noun)/quality of adj [i.e. other party might think there is a hidden purpose/something unordinary to it]
when used with a verb -> denial that action was done for a certain purpose or to just say that no action was taken and implying vagueness
connection: 別に + ... + ない

examples.japanese: それは別に悪いことでもない。
examples.english: That's not particularly a bad thing.
examples.pronunciation: sore wa betsu ni warui koto demo nai.

examples.japanese: 別に彼のために作ったわけではない。
examples.english: It’s not like I made this for him.
examples.pronunciation: betsu ni kare no tame ni tsukutta wake dewa nai.

examples.japanese: この仕事には別に期限はない。
examples.english: There is no time limit in particular for completing this job.
examples.pronunciation: kono shigoto ni wa betsu ni kigen wa nai.

examples.japanese: 君は別に悪いことをしている訳ではない。
examples.english: It's not like you're doing anything wrong.
examples.pronunciation: kimi wa betsu ni warui koto o shite iru wake dewa nai.

examples.japanese: ただ困っている人を手伝いたいだけだよ。別に彼のことが好きではない。
examples.english: I just want to help people who need help. It's not like I like him or anything.
examples.pronunciation: tada komatte iru hito o tetsudaitai dake dayo. Betsu ni kare no koto ga suki dewa nai.

examples.japanese: 私は結構です。別に欲しい物は無い。
examples.english: I'm good. It's not like there's anything I want.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa kekkou desu. betsu ni hoshii mono wa nai.

examples.japanese: 私は別に彼女が大嫌いなわけではありませんよ。
examples.english: It’s not that I really dislike her.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa betsu ni kanojo ga daikirai na wake dewa arimasen yo.

examples.japanese: いいよ。私は別に給料は無くても構わない。
examples.english: It's OK. I don't really mind if I don't have a salary.
examples.pronunciation: ii yo. watashi wa betsu ni kyuuryou wa nakutemo kamawanai.

meaning: considering; comparatively; relatively; unexpectedly; unusually ~
related:
considering...:
にしては
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
compares to specific or usual situation
often used when expatations were not met (either better or worse than expected)
connection: N / phrase / sentence + 割に

examples.japanese: 彼は年のわりに元気だ。
examples.english: He is in good shape for his age.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa toshi no wari ni genki da.

examples.japanese: このカメラは高いわりに、よく売れる。
examples.english: This camera sells unexpectedly well considering the high price.
examples.pronunciation: kono kamera wa takai wari ni, yoku ureru.

examples.japanese: このコンピューター、中古のわりに高くない？
examples.english: Isn't this computer a bit expensive for being used?
examples.pronunciation: kono konpyuutaa, chuuko no wari ni takakunai?

examples.japanese: 今日は休日のわりに人が少ないね。
examples.english: There are unusually few people today considering it's a holiday.
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa kyuujitsu no wari ni hito ga sukunai ne.

examples.japanese: 彼女は年の割には若く見える。
examples.english: She looks young for her age.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa toshi no wari ni wa wakaku mieru.

examples.japanese: この寿司は高い割にまずい。
examples.english: This sushi is bad for the price.
examples.pronunciation: kono sushi wa takai wari ni mazui.

examples.japanese: お金がないと言っている割に、よく旅行するね。
examples.english: Although you say you don't have any money, you seem to travel a lot.
examples.pronunciation: okane ga nai to itteiru wari ni, yoku ryokou suru ne.

examples.japanese: テストの勉強をしなかった割には、よく出来た。
examples.english: Considering you did not study for the test, you did well.
examples.pronunciation: tesuto no benkyou o shinakatta wari ni wa, yoku dekita.

meaning: do something together
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
indicates action that happens mutually and involve two parties doing something together or to each other
connection: V（ます形）ます + 合う
usageJapanese: お互いに〜する

examples.japanese: 私たちはその件について話し合いましょう。
examples.english: Let's discuss together regarding that matter.
examples.pronunciation: watashi tachi wa sono ken ni tsuite hanashi aimashou.

examples.japanese: 2人は愛し合っている。
examples.english: Those 2 are in love with each other.
examples.pronunciation: futari wa aishiatteiru.

examples.japanese: チームだから、困った時は助け合いましょう。
examples.english: We are a team so whenever someone is in trouble, let's help each other!
examples.pronunciation: chiimu da kara, komatta toki wa tasukeai mashou.

examples.japanese: 私たちはお互い助け合うべきだ。
examples.english: We should help each other.
examples.pronunciation: watashi tachi wa otagai ni tasuke au beki da.

examples.japanese: 彼女は仲間とうまく付き合っている。
examples.english: She gets along well with her peers.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa nakama to umaku tsukiatte iru.

examples.japanese: このプロジェクトが完成したら、ゆっくり休めるから頑張り合いましょう！
examples.english: Once this projects is finished we can rest easy, so let's work hard together to finish!
examples.pronunciation: kono purojekuto ga kansei shitara, yukkuri yasumeru kara ganbari aimashou!

examples.japanese: 問題が起きたときは、どうすべきかみんなで話し合いましょう。
examples.english: Let's discuss together with everyone on what we should do when a problem occurs.
examples.pronunciation: mondai ga okita toki wa, dou subeki ka minna de hanashi aimashou.

examples.japanese: その二人はいつも競い合っている。
examples.english: Those 2 are always competing with each other.
examples.pronunciation: sono futari wa itsumo kisoi atteiru.

examples.japanese: 隣の人と相談し合って答えを考えてください。
examples.english: Discuss with the person next to you and think about the answer.
examples.pronunciation: tonari no hito to soudan shi atte kotae o kangaete kudasai.

meaning: suitable for ~
related: 向け
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 向き

examples.japanese: この本は初心者向きである。
examples.english: This book is suitable for beginners.
examples.pronunciation: kono hon wa shoshinsha muki de aru.

examples.japanese: その映画は子供向きだ。
examples.english: This movie is suitable for children.
examples.pronunciation: kono eiga wa kodomo muki da.

examples.japanese: このスキー場は初心者向きです。
examples.english: This ski resort is suitable for beginners.
examples.pronunciation: kono sukii jou wa shoshinsha muki desu.

examples.japanese: このプレゼントは10歳の女の子向きだ。
examples.english: This present is suitable for a 10 year old girl.
examples.pronunciation: kono purezento wa 10 sai no onna no ko muki da.

examples.japanese: 若い女性向きの小説を書いています。
examples.english: I'm writing a short novel for young women.
examples.pronunciation: wakai josei muki no shousetsu o kaiteimasu.

examples.japanese: こういう本は若い読者向きではない。
examples.english: These kinds of books are not suitable for young readers.
examples.pronunciation: kouiu hon wa wakai dokusha uki dewa nai.

examples.japanese: この食堂にはランチは安くて量が多く、学生向きだ。
examples.english: This cafeteria's lunch is suitable for students as it is cheap with large portions.
examples.pronunciation: kono shokudou ni wa ranchi wa yasuku te ryou ga ooku, gakusei muki da.

examples.japanese: 彼は青少年向きの本をたくさん持っている。
examples.english: He has a lot of books made for young people.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa seishounen muki no hon o takusan motteiru.

meaning: intended for; aimed at ~
related: 向き
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
初心者 = shoshinsha = beginners
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 向け

examples.japanese: 初心者向けのコースがありますか。
examples.english: Do you have a course for beginners?
examples.pronunciation: shoshinsha muke no koosu ga arimasu ka.

examples.japanese: この映画は子供向けなので、大人は見てもつまらない。
examples.english: That movie is targeted for children, so it will be boring for adults who watch it.
examples.pronunciation: kono eiga wa kodomo muke nano de, otona wa mitemo tsumaranai.

examples.japanese: この家は、部屋が多くて、ファミリー向けだ。
examples.english: This house has a lot of rooms and is targeted towards families.
examples.pronunciation: kono ie wa, heya ga ookute, famirii muke da.

examples.japanese: 最近は、男性向けの料理教室が人気だ。
examples.english: Lately, cooking classes for men are popular.
examples.pronunciation: saikin wa dansei muke no ryouri kyoushitsu ga ninki da.

examples.japanese: ピアノ初心者向けのいい曲を知りませんか。
examples.english: Do you know any good songs suitable for beginner piano players?
examples.pronunciation: piano shoshinsha muke no ii kyoku o shiri masen ka?

examples.japanese: 子供向けの絵本コーナーは、１３番の棚にあります。
examples.english: The children's picture book corner is at shelf number 13.
examples.pronunciation: kodomo muke no ehon koonaa wa, 13 ban no tana ni arimasu.

examples.japanese: それは初心者向けの教科書です。
examples.english: That is a textbook aimed for beginners.
examples.pronunciation: Sore wa shoshinsha muke no kyoukasha desu.

examples.japanese: これは留学生向けに書かれた本です。
examples.english: This book was written for study abroad students.
examples.pronunciation: Kore wa ryuugakusei muke ni kakareta hon desu .

id: grammar:n3:数量 + は（すうりょう + は）
word: 数量 + は（すうりょう + は）
meaning: at least ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 数量 + は
usageJapanese: 少なくとも

examples.japanese: 今日はゆっくり休むと言ったけど、１時間は仕事をしました。
examples.english: I said I would rest today, but I still worked for at least an hour or so.
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa yukkuri yasumu to itta kedo, 1 jikan wa shigoto o shimashita.

examples.japanese: この映画は面白かったので、３回は見ました。
examples.english: I watched this movie at least three times now because it was interesting
examples.pronunciation: kono eiga wa omoshiro katta node, 3 kai wa mimashita.

examples.japanese: 毎年、１回は海外へ旅行します。
examples.english: Every year, I go travel abroad at least once.
examples.pronunciation: maitoshi, ikkai wa kaigai e ryokou shimasu.

examples.japanese: 毎日7時間は寝るようにしています。
examples.english: I make sure to sleep at least 7 hours every day.
examples.pronunciation: mainichi 7 jikan wa neru you ni shite imasu.

examples.japanese: あまり時間がないだが、毎週一回はボランティア活動をしています。
examples.english: I don't have much time, but I do volunteer activities at least once a week.
examples.pronunciation: amari jikan ga nai daga, maishuu ikkai wa borantia katsudou o shite imasu.

examples.japanese: 健康のために、毎日30分は運動をします。
examples.english: For my health, I workout at least 30 minutes every day.
examples.pronunciation: kenkou no tame ni, mainichi 30 pun wa undou o shimasu.

examples.japanese: テストは難しかったですが、80点はあると思います。
examples.english: The test was difficult, but I think I was able to get at least 80 points.
examples.pronunciation: tesuto wa muzukashikatta desu ga, 80 ten wa aru to omoimasu.

examples.japanese: 仕事で忙しくても、一日3回はご飯をちゃんと食べます。
examples.english: Even if I am busy at work, I make sure to eat 3 meals every day.
examples.pronunciation: shigoto de isogashikute mo, ichinichi 3 kai wa gohan o chanto tabemasu.

meaning: while; during; in the middle of ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
停電 ていでん blackout
connection:
Vている + 最中に
Nの + 最中に
usageJapanese:
〜しているときに / ちょうど〜中に
状態動詞や瞬間動詞と一緒に使うことができない。
（例. 住んでいる最中に地震があった）

examples.japanese: 食事の最中に大きな地震が起こった。
examples.english: During dinner there was a big earthquake.
examples.pronunciation: shokuji no saichuu ni ookina jishin ga okotta.

examples.japanese: 私はテニスの最中に自分で怪我をしてしまった。
examples.english: I hurt myself while I was playing tennis.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa tenisu no saichuu ni jibun de kega o shite shimatta.

examples.japanese: 私はパリを旅行している最中に彼女に会いました。
examples.english: I met her while I was traveling in Paris.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa pari o ryokou shiteiru saichuu ni kanojo ni aimashita.

examples.japanese: 会議をしている最中に、携帯電話が鳴った。
examples.english: My cell phone started to ring in the middle of the meeting.
examples.pronunciation: kaigi o shiteiru saichuu ni keitai denwa ga natta.

examples.japanese: 彼女は嵐の真っ最中に家を出た。
examples.english: She went out of the house right in the middle of a storm.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa arashi no massaichuu ni ie o deta.

examples.japanese: みんなが大笑いの最中彼が会議室に戻ってきた。
examples.english: He returned to the meeting room just as everyone burst out laughing. In the middle of a big laughter, he returned to the meeting room
examples.pronunciation: minna ga oowarai no saichuu kare ga kaigishitsu ni modotte kita.

examples.japanese: 彼の声が出なくなってしまったのはテレビ番組で歌っている最中でした。
examples.english: The time he unfortunately lost his voice was when he was singing on a television show.
examples.pronunciation: kare no koe ga denaku natteshimatta no wa terebi bangumi de utatteiru saichuu deshita.

meaning: -like; -looking; -looked; tending to ~
related:
tendency/(of personality/appearance/conduct) = seems like:
っぽい
そうだ
そうに / そうな
みたいだ
ようだ
ような気がする
そうだ
みたいな
みたいに
ようにみえる
とみえる
がち
に見える
まるで
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
寝不足 ねぶそく sleep deprivation
新入社員 しんにゅうしゃいん new employee
緊張 きんちょう nervous
顔 かお face
残業 ざんぎょう overtime
when combined with a noun one can see that the meaning is that whatever the noun describes is not reality yet but it tends towards it.
connection:
V（ます形）ます + 気味
N + 気味
usageJapanese:
いつもと違って、少し〜の感じがする
ネガティブな言葉として使われる。

examples.japanese: 彼は人の名前を忘れ気味である。
examples.english: He is apt to forget people's names.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa hito no namae o wasure gimi de aru.

examples.japanese: 昨夜、彼は風邪気味だった。
examples.english: He had a bit of a cold last night.
examples.pronunciation: sakuya, kare wa kaze gimi datta.

examples.japanese: 風邪気味で、熱っぽいんだ。
examples.english: I feel like I'm catching a cold, and I'm a bit feverish.
examples.pronunciation: kaze gimi de, netsu ppoi nda.

examples.japanese: 残業続きで疲れ気味だ。
examples.english: I'm feeling a bit tired due to continued overtime work.
examples.pronunciation: zangyou tsuzuki de tsukare gimi da.

examples.japanese: 新入社員は緊張気味の顔をしています。
examples.english: The new employee looks a little bit nervous.
examples.pronunciation: shinnyuu shain wa kinchou gimi no kao o shiteimasu.

examples.japanese: 彼女は遠慮気味にそう言った。
examples.english: She said that hesitantly.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa enryo gimi ni sou itta.

examples.japanese: 私も最近太り気味なので、運動しないといけません。
examples.english: I also tend to gain weight recently so I have to exercise.
examples.pronunciation: watashi mo saikin futori gimi nano de, undou shinaito ikemasen.

examples.japanese: 本日の株価は下がり気味です。
examples.english: The stock prices are on the low side today.
examples.pronunciation: honjitsu no kabuka wa sagari gimi desu.

id: grammar:n3:決して～ない（けっして～ない）
word: 決して～ない（けっして～ない）
meaning: never; by no means ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 決して + ... + ない
connectionSourcePoint: 決して～ない（けっして～ない）

examples.japanese: あなたのことは決して忘れません。
examples.english: I could never forget you.
examples.pronunciation: anata no koto wa kesshite wasuremasen.

examples.japanese: 私は決して夢をあきらめない。
examples.english: I’ll never give up on my dream.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa kesshite yume o akiramenai.

examples.japanese: この部屋には決して入ってはいけませんよ。
examples.english: You must never enter this room under any circumstances.
examples.pronunciation: kono heya ni wa kesshite haitte wa ikemasen yo.

examples.japanese: 私はタバコは決して吸いません。
examples.english: I definitely do not smoke cigarettes.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa tabako wa kesshite suimasen.

examples.japanese: 決してあなたは一人じゃありません。いつでも私に相談してくださいね。
examples.english: You are never alone. Feel free to talk to me at any time.
examples.pronunciation: kesshite anata wa hitori jaarimasen. itsu demo watashi ni soudan shite kudasai ne.

examples.japanese: 社長には、決して失礼な言葉を言ってはいけません。
examples.english: You must by no means say anything disrespectful to the company president.
examples.pronunciation: shachou ni wa, kesshite shitsurei na kotoba o itte wa ikemasen.

examples.japanese: あの人がしたことは危ないですから、みなさんは決して真似しないでください。
examples.english: What that person did is dangerous so please everyone, definitely do not copy them.
examples.pronunciation: ano hito ga shita koto wa abunai desu kara, minasan wa kesshite mane shinaide kudasai.

examples.pronunciation: ima kara watashi ga hanasu koto wa totemo taisetsu desu kara, kesshite wasurenai youni shite kudasai ne.

meaning: change noun into na-adjective (-ish; -like; -ish)
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
adjective+的 is a representative for (all) nouns that match the adjective and context

examples.japanese: 私は基本的になんでも食べます。

examples.pronunciation: sono kangae kata wa ronri teki ni dame da to omoimasu yo.

examples.japanese: 私は仕事を辞めてから精神的にとても楽になった。

examples.pronunciation: watashi wa shigoto o yamete kara seishin teki ni totemo raku ni natta.

examples.japanese: もっと具体的なアイディアを出して下さい。
examples.english: Please give me more specific ideas.
examples.pronunciation: motto gutai teki na aidea o dashite kudasai.

examples.japanese: その言葉は一般的に会話で使われていないよ。
examples.english: That word is generally not used in conversation.
examples.pronunciation: sono kotoba wa ippan teki ni kaiwa de tsukawarete inai yo.

examples.japanese: 日本は経済的に強力な国家になった。
examples.english: Japan has economically become a powerful nation.
examples.pronunciation: nihon wa keizai teki ni kyouryoku na kokka ni natta.

meaning: to do something again; to do over
notes:

・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
作文 = さくぶん =　essay

examples.japanese: この作文はミスが多いから、書き直したほうがいいよ。

examples.pronunciation: kare ni denwa o kake naosu you ni onegai shite moraemasu ka.

examples.japanese: 私はその仕事をやり直すことになる。

examples.pronunciation: watashi wa sono shigoto o yarinaosu koto ni naru.

examples.english: This house is starting to get old, so I am thinking to have it rebuilt.
examples.pronunciation: ie ga furukute kita node, tatenaosou to omotte imasu.

examples.english: You should rewrite that methodology so that it is easier to understand.
examples.pronunciation: anata wa sono houhou o wakari yasuku kakinaosu beki desu.

examples.japanese: 彼は人生をもう一度やり直す決心をした。
examples.english: He decided to start his life over again.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa jinsei o mou ichido yarinaosu kesshin o shita.

examples.english: Please review your proposal one more time.
examples.pronunciation: sono teian wa mou ichido minaoshite kudasai.
